title: pulicaria vulgaris subsp. dentata
intro: Pulicaria vulgaris subsp. dentata, also known as Greater Stickelback or Sticky Dock, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant's medicinal properties are said to provide various health benefits, including the relief of respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations, as well as acting as a natural diuretic. Pulicaria vulgaris subsp. dentata is often prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve for topical use, with some herbal practitioners recommending it for the treatment of wounds, eczema, and other skin conditions.
